rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex blocks or fixing syntax errors without changing unrelated architecture
Use only valid AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Minimize repairs to only the necessary changes required to resolve validator diagnostics
Preserve existing architecture and only modify the necessary parts to fix the diagnostic
Use visible identifiers and valid AGREE syntax when creating new clauses
Avoid using subcomponent paths or implementation-level references in component type contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Use const for constants and eq for equations in AGREE contracts
Guarantees should be placed in the appropriate annex scope based on the reference level
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture or components to fix validator diagnostics
Avoid using invalid or unresolved identifiers in guarantees or equations
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
